meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS MVP fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from focusing on features rather than user friction. By building a high-conversion onboarding flow first, you force yourself to define the user's 'Aha!' moment. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to iterate on the logic, turning a blank slate into a revenue-generating asset.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the founder's greatest adversary. You have the ambition to build, but the 'no project ideas' trap keeps you cycle-looping in research and procrastination. The truth is, you don't need a groundbreaking invention; you need a process that turns small, observable user pain points into a repeatable onboarding experience that captures value from day one.

Great SaaS products aren't built on genius, but on the relentless optimization of the user journey. By focusing on onboarding, you stop building 'features' and start building 'solutions.' This guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to move you from analysis paralysis to a functional, deployable MVP that proves your business model before you write a single line of unnecessary code.

steps.title: Define the User's 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the exact action that proves value to your user. If they don't reach this in 60 seconds, your project is already dead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a 3-step onboarding flow for a [Insert Niche] SaaS that delivers immediate value. Map the user journey from landing page to the core feature.

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code for your onboarding flow, ensuring state management is handled correctly from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular React component structure for a multi-step onboarding wizard. Include state management for user progress and form validation.

steps.title: Implement Frictionless Data Collection
steps.description: Don't ask for everything at once. Build a progressive form that asks for only what is necessary to get the user to the next step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a progressive form schema for onboarding. Only ask for essential data points and ensure the UI is optimized for high conversion rates.

steps.title: Connect to Backend Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wire up your frontend to a backend service, ensuring user progress is saved and retrievable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the API service layer to handle user onboarding state. Include endpoints for saving progress and final submission.

steps.title: Automate Success Signals
steps.description: Set up triggers to notify you when a user completes onboarding, allowing you to manually reach out and validate their experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a webhook integration that sends an alert to Slack/Discord when a user completes the onboarding flow successfully.

pro_tips: Kill the 'Sign Up' button; use a 'Get Started' CTA to reduce cognitive friction.
Never ask for more than one piece of information per step in your wizard.
Show a progress bar to leverage the Zeigarnik effect—users feel compelled to finish what they start.
Use Claude Code to run automated unit tests on your onboarding state machine before deploying.
A/B test your first onboarding step's headline every 48 hours until conversion hits 30%.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, spend 48 hours manually onboarding your first 10 users. Observe where they drop off, iterate your flow, and then move to building the core feature set.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the strategic mindset to stop waiting for inspiration and start shipping a product that users actually want to pay for. Stop overthinking the 'perfect' idea and start optimizing the 'perfect' user experience. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
